title: claimEarned pays fixed rewards immediately and can be repeated indefinitely for the same stake
claim: claimEarned iterates over every stake and adds amount * apr / 10000 without checking whether endstakeAt has passed and without marking any reward as settled. The function only updates stakeAt, but stakeAt is never used by earnedToken, unstake, or claimEarned to determine eligibility, so the same stake can generate the same payout on every call, including before lock expiry.
impact: Any staker can drain the pooled staking token balance by repeatedly calling claimEarned, extracting rewards that were never earned and exhausting funds needed to repay other users' principal and rewards.
paths: Create any stake in any plan and call claimEarned immediately, before the plan's lock duration ends.
Call claimEarned again in later transactions; each call recomputes the same fixed reward from the unchanged stake amount and transfers it again.

title: Rewards claimed via claimEarned are paid again during unstake
claim: claimEarned does not reduce stake principal or record that a stake's reward has already been consumed for later withdrawals. unstake separately recomputes rewards from the amount being withdrawn once endstakeAt has passed, so a user who has already claimed can still receive the same reward again when unstaking the same matured principal.
impact: Even if repeated claimEarned abuse were somehow constrained, matured positions can still be double-paid through the normal claim-then-unstake flow, accelerating insolvency of the staking pool.
paths: Wait until a stake matures, call claimEarned once, then call unstake for the same amount.
claimEarned transfers the APR reward first; unstake later recomputes amount * apr / 10000 for the withdrawn matured amount and transfers that reward again.

title: Owner can arbitrarily sweep staked tokens and leave the pool insolvent
claim: User deposits are held directly in the contract's stakingToken balance, and penaltyWithdraw lets the owner transfer out any amount strictly below that balance without reserving assets for outstanding principal, matured withdrawals, or promised rewards. No accounting fence prevents owner withdrawals from consuming user funds.
impact: The owner can rug most of the staking pool, causing user unstakes and reward claims to fail or become undercollateralized.
paths: Users stake tokens, which accumulate in the contract balance.
The owner calls penaltyWithdraw with nearly the full token balance.
Subsequent unstake or claimEarned calls no longer have enough tokens backing them.

title: Core staking flows ignore ERC20 return values and can mutate accounting after failed token transfers
claim: Although the contract imports SafeERC20, stake, unstake, claimEarned, and emergencyWithdraw use raw transfer/transferFrom calls and do not check their boolean return values. For tokens that return false instead of reverting, these functions can proceed after a failed token movement and still record stake creation, reduce user stake amounts, or account rewards as if payment succeeded.
impact: With a false-returning or otherwise non-compliant staking token, users can lose principal or reward entitlement in contract state without actually receiving tokens, and the protocol can record stake positions that were never funded.
paths: If transferFrom returns false in stake, the function can still append a new stake entry using the observed balance delta, potentially creating a zero-funded or underfunded position.
If transfer returns false in unstake, claimEarned, or emergencyWithdraw, the function can complete after decrementing stake amounts or incrementing reward totals even though no tokens were delivered.
